concept.name: Repair-Closed Effect (RCE)
concept.definition: Importance of component c = the marginal total effect of ablating c JOINTLY with its 'repair closure' S(c): grow S greedily by adding the downstream component whose direct effect increases most when c is ablated (measured by patching), until the marginal effect TE(S∪{c}) − TE(S) stabilizes within tolerance ε. Report RCE(c) = the stabilized marginal effect, plus repair depth k(c) = |S(c)|. All quantities are standard activation-patching runs; the only new machinery is the closure loop.
concept.prediction: On GPT-2-small IOI: (1) RCE rank-ordering of attention heads is protocol-invariant — Spearman correlation across zero/mean/resample ablation > 0.9, where raw total effect gives < 0.6 (the flip documented in arXiv:2402.15390); (2) k(c) ≤ 3 for the name-mover heads, and the closure sets recovered are exactly the known backup name movers, without being told about them.
concept.reduces_to: The 'backup heads' analysis in the IOI paper (Wang et al. 2022) turned from a one-off observation into an operator; also a Shapley value with coalitions restricted to downstream repairers.

test.design: Preregistered in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/rce_ioi/PREREG.md (committed be601bb BEFORE the run). Operationalization: gpt2-small, 16 IOI prompts (1 template, 8 single-token name pairs x ABBA/BABA), effect = clean minus ablated logit-diff(IO,S); three protocols = zero / batch-mean / resample (roll-1) overwrite of hook_z at all positions. RCE(h) = effect of ablating the repair-closure of h: greedily add the head whose direct-logit-attribution most compensates (sign-matched, thresh 0.1 LD units, cap 6). Eval subset = top-12 heads by mean |raw effect| across protocols, union the 3 name movers. Decision rule, fixed: P1 SUPPORTED iff min pairwise Spearman(RCE) >= 0.9 AND Spearman(raw) <= 0.6 on the same subset; REFUTED iff Spearman(RCE) <= Spearman(raw) or < 0.7. P2 SUPPORTED iff for seeds 9.9/9.6/10.0: k <= 3 and all recovered repairers in the known backup+negative name-mover set; REFUTED iff k > 3 or members outside it. Oracle gate before any verdict.
test.numbers: Both sub-predictions were numerically ANTI-supported: (1) RCE min pairwise Spearman = 0.187 (zero-mean 0.19, zero-resample 0.64, mean-resample 0.21) vs raw = 0.379 on the same subset — RCE is LESS protocol-invariant than raw total effect, opposite of the predicted >0.9 vs <0.6; raw mean-resample already agrees at 0.98 (all-144: 0.905), only zero deviates. (2) Closures of name movers 9.9 and 9.6 hit the k=6 cap under all three protocols (predicted k<=3) and include heads outside the known backup set (11.1, 11.6) — though they heavily overlap it: 10.2/10.6/10.10/11.2 (backup movers) and 10.7/11.10 (negative movers) were recovered unprompted, and 10.0's closure {11.10} or {11.10,11.2} is fully inside the known set with k<=2. Under the prereg rules these numbers read REFUTED, but the verdict is capped at INCONCLUSIVE by the preregistered sanity-gate chain (see sanity). Runtime ~37 min CPU. Artifacts: research_cycle/experiments/rce_ioi/{PREREG.md,exp.py,result.json,sanity2.py,sanity3.py,sanity2.json,sanity3.json,SANITY_ADDENDUM.md}, commits be601bb/89b24a2.
test.sanity: Mixed — and this is why the verdict is inconclusive, not refuted. Passed (unconfounded machinery checks): clean logit-diff 3.889 with 100% IOI accuracy; DLA recovered exactly the three known name movers as top-3 unprompted (9.9=3.45 max, 9.6=1.27, 10.0=0.76); hook verified mechanically (DLA(9.9)=0.0000 under its own zero-ablation). Failed (phenomenon-confounded controls): the preregistered oracle "zero-abl 9.9 moves LD >= 0.5" gave 0.117, and two escalation controls (joint zero-abl of all 3 name movers: LD moved -0.66 i.e. UP; joint mean-abl: drop only 0.399 < 1.0) — each failure is itself strong self-repair, the concept's own premise, so no unconfounded end-to-end positive control exists in this design. Escalation rules were written down BEFORE each supplementary run (SANITY_ADDENDUM.md) and the final preregistered rule "hook check passes + total-effect control fails -> INCONCLUSIVE, stop" was honored. Honest read: refuted-leaning inconclusive; a redesign needs a repair-free positive control (e.g. path-patching the direct path) before the refutation can be claimed cleanly. Limits: gpt2-small only, one template, 16 prompts, all-position ablation, one closure operationalization.

concept.name: Compensation Matrix K
concept.definition: Repair as a first-class measurable object: K[c,d] = DE_d(model with c ablated) − DE_d(clean model), the change in component d's direct effect induced by removing c. Estimable for all pairs with one attribution-patching backward pass per ablated c (O(n) not O(n²) forward passes). Effective importance under redundancy: EI(c) = TE(c) + Σ_d K[c,d]·τ_d, where τ_d is d's clean transmission to the logit. The DE/TE gap is no longer noise — it is the row of K.
concept.prediction: On Pythia-410m with the prompt distribution of arXiv:2402.15390, the row-norm ‖K[c,·]‖₁ predicts each head's self-repair magnitude (DE − TE) with R² > 0.7 across heads; heads with row-norm ≈ 0 show DE ≈ TE within noise. Falsified if repair magnitude is uncorrelated with row-norm, i.e. repair is not attributable to identifiable downstream absorbers.
concept.reduces_to: Pairwise Shapley interaction indices / second-order Taylor terms of the ablation response; the self-repair paper already computes slices of K without naming it as the object.

test.design: Preregistered BEFORE running (committed 2d6f535, maevtica repo: research_cycle/experiments/comp_matrix_k/PREREG.md). Operationalization: pythia-160m (budget stand-in for 410m; deviation declared up front), 24 wikitext-103 prompts (stand-in for the Pile distribution of 2402.15390), ONE intervention = mean-ablation of each head's hook_z at the final position, metric = correct-next-token logit. K[c,d] = signed change in downstream component d's frozen-LN direct contribution (heads+MLPs of layers >l only — Pythia is parallel attn/MLP); DE, TE, ΔLN defined so the exact identity TE = DE − ΣK + ΔLN must close. 132 heads (layers 0-10). DECISION RULE (fixed): SUPPORTED iff OLS R² of repair=(DE−TE) on ‖K[c,·]‖₁ ≥ 0.7 AND ≥75% of bottom-decile-rownorm heads have |repair| ≤ 2·SEM; REFUTED iff R² < 0.3 (or R²<0.7 with clause failing); gates: closure oracle, effect-exists, and a phenomenon gate (≥10 heads with |repair|>0.02 and >2·SEM, else NO_PHENOMENON → inconclusive, not refuted).
test.numbers: Phenomenon gate FAILED by one head: 9 strong-repair heads vs preregistered ≥10 → verdict is INCONCLUSIVE by the pre-committed rule (not moved post hoc). The conditional evidence leans REFUTED, and in an interesting way: primary R² = 0.204 (below the 0.3 refute line had the gate passed), Pearson r = −0.45 — the correlation has the WRONG SIGN (bigger ℓ1 row mass → more anti-repair, not more repair); strong-heads-only r = −0.58. The signed sum ΣK correlates 0.71 with repair (partly by identity), but the ℓ1 row-norm does not — the compensation entries cancel heavily, exactly the escape route the concept denies. LN-scale term is comparable to the entire repair (ln_share ≈ 1.16), echoing 2402.15390's own finding that LayerNorm carries much of self-repair. The zero-rownorm clause alone did hold (84.6% of bottom-decile heads have DE≈TE within 2·SEM). Mean repair is negative (−0.0096): at this regime (160m, wikitext, mean-ablation at final position) anti-repair slightly dominates. Scope caveat: this does not execute the literal 410m/Pile/resample claim — self-repair is known to strengthen with model size, so 410m could differ; but the nearest cheap regime gives the ℓ1-row-norm predictor no support and a wrong-signed correlation. Artifacts committed (9daec91): exp.py, result.json, run.log, arrays.npz in research_cycle/experiments/comp_matrix_k/.
test.sanity: All oracles PASSED, so the null-ish result is real, not a broken pipeline: (1) exact-decomposition closure |(DE − ΣK + ΔLN) − TE| median 2.2e-6, p99 7.7e-6 across 132×24 head-prompt pairs — the hooks, frozen-LN math, and parallel-architecture downstream set are provably correct to float precision; (2) frozen-LN formula reproduces the model's clean logit to 1.9e-6; (3) ablations move logits: max |TE| = 0.225 ≥ 0.05 gate; (4) clean mean logprob −4.27 > −7 (model behaving); smoke test on one head ran before the full sweep. This is the check class that caught the earlier .norm bug — here it shows the measurement worked and the phenomenon itself is simply weak in this regime.

concept.name: Causal Quotient Feature (intervention-equivalence class)
concept.definition: Fix layer L and a behavior metric m (e.g. logit-diff on an eval set). Declare two perturbations equivalent, d1 ~ d2, iff max over eval contexts |m(h+d1) - m(h+d2)| <= epsilon. The representation of a concept is the equivalence class of the minimal-norm perturbation achieving a target behavioral shift, reported as (effective causal rank k = number of non-negligible singular values of the local perturbation-to-effect Jacobian, canonical representative = its top singular vector). 'Which direction IS the representation' becomes a category error: probe/steer/SAE vectors are different coset representatives of one quotient class.
concept.prediction: Computable: for concepts where probe direction, difference-of-means steering vector, and matched SAE decoder row have pairwise cosine < 0.6 (the field's current embarrassment), >= 90% of the squared norm of their pairwise DIFFERENCE vectors lies in the causal null space (bottom singular subspace of the perturbation-effect Jacobian), and projecting all three onto the top-k causal subspace raises pairwise cosines to >= 0.9. FALSIFIED if the difference vectors carry substantial causal effect (i.e., the three objects genuinely do different things rather than being null-space-shifted copies).
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction / DAS (Geiger et al.) and activation patching — risk it is causal mediation analysis wearing a quotient-space costume.

survivors.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)

concept.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)
concept.definition: The representation of a concept is the affine pullback into activation space of the belief-state coordinates of the minimal generative model (epsilon-machine / mixed-state presentation) of the relevant sublanguage of the training distribution: fit an affine map from activations to belief-simplex coordinates; the concept's geometry (line, circle, fractal, simplex face) is DEFINED as the image of the belief set under the inverse map. This converts the LRH from an hypothesis about models into a computable function of the data: linear features are exactly the concepts whose minimal process has a 1-simplex belief set.
concept.prediction: Pre-registered shape forecasting: pick >= 5 structured domains where the mixed-state geometry is computable before touching the network (e.g. base-12 clock arithmetic -> circle; a 3-state Mess3-style HMM -> fractal; a binary flag process -> line). Predict, in advance, the intrinsic dimension and topology (Betti numbers via persistent homology) of the activation manifold a transformer trained on that domain will exhibit for the concept. Match rate must beat chance with p < 0.01. FALSIFIED by a single clean case of a model reaching the same next-token loss while representing a provably-circular process on a line (or vice versa) — that would show model-internal factors, not data geometry, set the shape.
concept.reduces_to: Shai et al. belief-state fractal geometry itself — the only delta is promoting it from post-hoc explanation of one experiment to a forward-predictive definition of 'the representation'; if that promotion fails, it renamed nothing.

CONTROL calibration of this campaign's adversary+historian bar (cycle.workflow.js: schemas S_ADV L59-61 / S_HIST L62-65, prompts P.adversary L79 / P.historian L80, survive/kill gate L137-143), domain "mechanistic interpretability". Ran 2 known-real + 2 distractor concepts through the identical bar.

KNOWN-GOOD (both PASSED, as they should):

Gate: separable -> SURVIVES. Correct.
- Induction head (specific 2-head prefix-match/copy circuit; predicts ablation kills in-context learning + abrupt formation at a training-loss phase change). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T (copying/n-gram), separable_prediction=T. Gate: separable -> SURVIVES. Correct.

DISTRACTORS (both KILLED, as they should):
- "Neural resonance" (activations 'harmonize' -> understanding; circular, no failure boundary; = cross-layer activation correlation / CKA in a metaphor). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate -> KILLED. Correct.
- "Semantic embedding direction" (a direction whose addition changes concept output = CAV/TCAV/steering/ActAdd renamed; prediction IS the prior-art operation, nothing to separate). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate -> KILLED. Correct.

VERDICT: 2/2 good passed, 2/2 distractors killed -> the judges discriminate real MI concepts from vague/renamed ones on this sample; the campaign's keep/kill verdicts are trustworthy at this calibration point. This independently reproduces the prior control's result (run1_final.json).

LOAD-BEARING CAVEAT (the one real fragility to watch): both known-good concepts survive ONLY via the historian's separable_prediction=T escape hatch, NOT via the strict clearlyNovel path (both are name-level rediscoveries, so !is_rediscovery=F). By the gate, separable_prediction=T forces survival REGARDLESS of the adversary -- so the entire keep/kill discrimination rests on a single boolean from a single judge (the historian), and the adversary's is_rename flag does NOT block survival. This is the intended CRC-style design (don't let the armchair adversary pre-kill testable concepts), but it means the one failure mode that would leak a distractor is a too-generous historian stamping separable_prediction=T on a pure rename whose prediction matches prior art. In this sample the historian held the line correctly (granted to superposition/induction, withheld from resonance/semantic-direction), but I recommend: (1) treat any concept with adv.is_rename=T AND hist.separable_prediction=T as a manual review flag, since that combination alone can carry a rename to the teeth; (2) keep controlEvery low. Files: research_cycle/cycle.workflow.js, campaigns/night-2026-07-04/round1.json + run1_final.json.

concept.name: Gated Feature (direction x context contract)
concept.definition: A feature is a pair (v, g): direction v plus a sparse gate g — a predicate of <= 5 literals over co-occurring SAE-latent activity ('latent j active / inactive') — such that the mediation effect of patching along v conditional on g(x)=1 exceeds threshold theta, with g fit by decision tree / lasso on conditional-patching outcomes and validated on held-out inputs (precision >= 0.7). The four senses of 'feature' are predicted to coincide inside the gate and dissociate outside it; an ungated direction is not a feature, it is a marginal average over contexts.
concept.prediction: For >= 25% of SAE latents whose unconditional mediation effect falls in the bottom quintile of their layer, there exists a learnable gate (<= 5 literals, held-out precision >= 0.7) under which their mediation effect exceeds the layer median by >= 3x — i.e., most 'causally dead but interpretable' latents are context-marginalization artifacts. Directly computable with conditional activation patching. Falsified if conditional patching rescues < 5% of dead latents.
concept.reduces_to: Circuits / feature-interaction analysis (input-conditional feature effects in attribution graphs) — possibly just renaming 'a feature only matters inside its circuit' with a fitting procedure attached.

test.design: Preregistered in research_cycle/experiments/gated_feature/PREREG.md BEFORE running (thresholds fixed, committed to file). Model: gpt2 CPU, layer 7 (blocks.7.hook_resid_pre) with jbloom GPT2-Small SAE. Corpus: 120 fixed short prompts; context = prompt; BOS excluded. Sampled M=134 latents firing in >=25 prompts (fallback rule triggered as prereg allowed; reported). ONE intervention: per (latent, prompt) decoder-row ablation (subtract a_i*W_dec[i] at all firing positions); effect = max over post-first-fire positions of next-token KL(clean||ablated) [Amendment 1: run 1 read KL only at the last position and tripped the oracle; measurement fixed, decision rule untouched]. Dead set = bottom quintile of unconditional effect U_i. Gate family: conjunctions of <=5 literals over OTHER latents' prompt-level firing indicators (own activation excluded — trivial rescue banned); greedy forward selection on 60% train split. Rescue (held-out 40%): gate fires on >=3 test contexts, test precision >=0.7 for label [effect >= 3x layer median], and mean gated test effect >= 3x layer median. DECISION RULE (fixed pre-run): SUPPORTED if rescue_rate >= 0.25; REFUTED if < 0.05; else INCONCLUSIVE; shuffle control (effects permuted within latent, seed 2) demotes fake positives to INCONCLUSIVE_OVERFIT. Note: run artifacts existed from an earlier pass of this stage tonight; I verified the code implements the prereg line-by-line, then independently re-ran the measurement (diag.py) and reproduced every summary number exactly before accepting the verdict.
test.numbers: M=134 latents, 27 dead (bottom quintile). layer_median=0.000808 nats, 3x threshold=0.002424 nats. rescued = 0/27 -> rescue_rate = 0.0 (< 0.05 refutation bound; prediction needed >= 0.25). Shuffle control rescue = 0/27. Decisive diagnostic (independent re-run, exact reproduction of all run-2 numbers): only 4/27 dead latents have ANY context with effect >= 3x layer median, 0/27 have >=3 such contexts, 5 high-effect (latent,context) pairs total across all dead latents — the rescue criterion is unsatisfiable even by an oracle gate, so the failure is not the gate learner. Dead latents are quiet in essentially every context, not marginalization artifacts. Files: research_cycle/experiments/gated_feature/{PREREG.md, exp.py, exp2.py, diag.py, result.json, diag.json, eff_run2.npz} in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/.

Independent re-run reproduced oracle_max 0.36802 and oracle_topq_median 0.00608 exactly. The pipeline demonstrably detects real effects; the dead latents' zeros are measured zeros.

stress.where: Ablation/activation-patching as the field's causal ground truth — Hydra Effect (arXiv:2307.15771), Explorations of Self-Repair (arXiv:2402.15390)
stress.why: The core measurement assumes removing a component reveals its contribution, but downstream layers actively compensate (self-repair), so direct-effect and total-effect importance decorrelate. Results also flip between zero/mean/resample ablation, so 'importance of head X' is not a well-defined quantity. Practitioners patch by inventing new ablation protocols per paper instead of a concept of importance-under-redundancy. A concept like 'compensated importance' (importance measured jointly with predicted repair magnitude from downstream weights) would restore a single number.

concept.name: Perturbation-Normalized Response Curve (PNRC)
concept.definition: Importance is a one-dimensional curve, not a scalar: I_c(ε) = E[Δmetric | c's output is moved distance ε (residual-stream norm) along the direction from clean activation toward the counterfactual]. Zero-, mean-, and resample-ablation are re-cast as single points on this curve at their induced ε. Report the curve's initial slope dI/dε|₀ (linear importance) and its curvature/monotonicity (nonlinearity index). Computable with ~10 forward passes per component per prompt set.
concept.prediction: For each head in GPT-2-small, plotting the Δmetric of zero/mean/resample ablations against their measured ε collapses onto one monotone curve (R² > 0.9 for a majority of heads); the specific heads where protocols flip SIGN are exactly the heads whose fitted curve is non-monotone. If protocol disagreement persists after conditioning on ε (points at the same ε give different Δmetric), the representation is falsified.
concept.reduces_to: Attribution patching / AtP is exactly the ε→0 slope; this claims the useful object is the whole curve. Might just be renaming 'damage-response curves' / influence-function linearization plus a second-order term.

test.design: Files: /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/pnrc_ioi/{PREREG.md, exp.py, result.json, curves.npz, run.log}. Setup: gpt2-small CPU, 16 IOI prompts (1 template, ABBA+BABA), metric = mean logit-diff LD(IO−S). For each of the 144 heads: 3 protocols (zero / per-position batch-mean / roll-by-1 resample) x 3 scales alpha in {1/3, 2/3, 1} applied at hook_z all positions as z' = z + alpha(target − z), giving 9 (epsilon, deltaLD) points per head; epsilon = mean ||z'−z_clean|| / mean ||z_clean|| at the site (computed from clean cache). Collapse = pooled isotonic (PAV) fit of deltaLD on epsilon, both directions, best R2. Effect heads = max|delta| >= 0.05. Sign-flip = full-strength protocols with delta > +0.05 and < −0.05. PREREGISTERED RULE (fixed before run): P1 supported iff >=50% of effect heads have R2 > 0.9, refuted otherwise; P2 ("flip heads are EXACTLY the non-monotone heads") supported iff Jaccard(sign-flip set, non-collapse set) >= 0.75, refuted iff <= 0.25; overall SUPPORTED iff both, REFUTED iff either refuted. Oracle amendment (written after a 1-head smoke, before the main run): the original |dLD(9.9,zero)|>=0.5 control is confounded by self-repair (rce_ioi sibling proved hook applies yet LD barely moves), replaced by pipeline checks: clean LD>1 & acc>0.9, hook-mechanics (cached z of 9.9 == 0 under its own ablation), and some name-mover full intervention moving LD >= 0.5.
test.numbers: Oracle: clean_LD=3.889, acc=1.00, mech_norm=0.0, max name-mover full effect=1.338 -> oracle OK. Effect heads 117/144 (27 flat). P1: collapse fraction R2>0.9 = 0.803 (94/117), median R2 = 0.998 -> P1 SUPPORTED (majority collapses). P2: sign-flip heads = {10.1, 9.3} only; non-collapse heads = 23 (incl. 9.9 R2=0.16, 11.3 R2=0.13, 10.0, 10.10, 11.2, 11.10, 4.4, 3.5...); Jaccard(flip, non-monotone) = 0.042 (<=0.25) -> P2 REFUTED. One sign-flip head (9.3, R2=0.929) even collapses, directly contradicting "flip => non-monotone". The prediction's own falsification clause fired on the circuit-critical heads: 21+ heads show protocol disagreement at matched epsilon WITHOUT any sign flip — e.g. head 9.9 at eps~0.31-0.33 gives deltaLD −0.130 (zero) vs −0.585 (resample), and −0.128 (zero, eps 0.667) vs −0.923 (resample, eps 0.615). Median matched-eps gap = 0.10 of head range (fine for typical heads, catastrophic for name movers). OVERALL preregistered verdict: REFUTED (~22 min run, 1296 batched forwards).
test.sanity: Positive control passed on all three amended checks: clean LD 3.889 with 100% IO-vs-S accuracy; hook mechanically applies (cached hook_z of head 9.9 has norm exactly 0.0 under its own zero-ablation — verified the caching hook fires AFTER the intervention hook in a separate ordering test); end-to-end sensitivity real (resample of 9.9 moves LD by −1.338, well over the 0.5 gate). Also unit-tested the PAV/isotonic R2 on known shapes (perfect monotone -> 1.0 both directions, sine -> 0.80, shuffled input handled). Note the original preregistered oracle (zero-abl 9.9 total effect >= 0.5) FAILED at 0.117 — diagnosed via the sibling rce_ioi SANITY_ADDENDUM as self-repair confounding the control, not a broken pipeline; oracle was amended transparently BEFORE the main run with the decision rules untouched.

concept.name: Compensation Matrix K
concept.definition: Repair as a first-class measurable object: K[c,d] = DE_d(model with c ablated) − DE_d(clean model), the change in component d's direct effect induced by removing c. Estimable for all pairs with one attribution-patching backward pass per ablated c (O(n) not O(n²) forward passes). Effective importance under redundancy: EI(c) = TE(c) + Σ_d K[c,d]·τ_d, where τ_d is d's clean transmission to the logit. The DE/TE gap is no longer noise — it is the row of K.
concept.prediction: On Pythia-410m with the prompt distribution of arXiv:2402.15390, the row-norm ‖K[c,·]‖₁ predicts each head's self-repair magnitude (DE − TE) with R² > 0.7 across heads; heads with row-norm ≈ 0 show DE ≈ TE within noise. Falsified if repair magnitude is uncorrelated with row-norm, i.e. repair is not attributable to identifiable downstream absorbers.
concept.reduces_to: Pairwise Shapley interaction indices / second-order Taylor terms of the ablation response; the self-repair paper already computes slices of K without naming it as the object.

test.design: Preregistered BEFORE running (committed 2d6f535, maevtica repo: research_cycle/experiments/comp_matrix_k/PREREG.md). Operationalization: pythia-160m (budget stand-in for 410m; deviation declared up front), 24 wikitext-103 prompts (stand-in for the Pile distribution of 2402.15390), ONE intervention = mean-ablation of each head's hook_z at the final position, metric = correct-next-token logit. K[c,d] = signed change in downstream component d's frozen-LN direct contribution (heads+MLPs of layers >l only — Pythia is parallel attn/MLP); DE, TE, ΔLN defined so the exact identity TE = DE − ΣK + ΔLN must close. 132 heads (layers 0-10). DECISION RULE (fixed): SUPPORTED iff OLS R² of repair=(DE−TE) on ‖K[c,·]‖₁ ≥ 0.7 AND ≥75% of bottom-decile-rownorm heads have |repair| ≤ 2·SEM; REFUTED iff R² < 0.3 (or R²<0.7 with clause failing); gates: closure oracle, effect-exists, and a phenomenon gate (≥10 heads with |repair|>0.02 and >2·SEM, else NO_PHENOMENON → inconclusive, not refuted).
test.numbers: Phenomenon gate FAILED by one head: 9 strong-repair heads vs preregistered ≥10 → verdict is INCONCLUSIVE by the pre-committed rule (not moved post hoc). The conditional evidence leans REFUTED, and in an interesting way: primary R² = 0.204 (below the 0.3 refute line had the gate passed), Pearson r = −0.45 — the correlation has the WRONG SIGN (bigger ℓ1 row mass → more anti-repair, not more repair); strong-heads-only r = −0.58. The signed sum ΣK correlates 0.71 with repair (partly by identity), but the ℓ1 row-norm does not — the compensation entries cancel heavily, exactly the escape route the concept denies. LN-scale term is comparable to the entire repair (ln_share ≈ 1.16), echoing 2402.15390's own finding that LayerNorm carries much of self-repair. The zero-rownorm clause alone did hold (84.6% of bottom-decile heads have DE≈TE within 2·SEM). Mean repair is negative (−0.0096): at this regime (160m, wikitext, mean-ablation at final position) anti-repair slightly dominates. Scope caveat: this does not execute the literal 410m/Pile/resample claim — self-repair is known to strengthen with model size, so 410m could differ; but the nearest cheap regime gives the ℓ1-row-norm predictor no support and a wrong-signed correlation. Artifacts committed (9daec91): exp.py, result.json, run.log, arrays.npz in research_cycle/experiments/comp_matrix_k/.
test.sanity: All oracles PASSED, so the null-ish result is real, not a broken pipeline: (1) exact-decomposition closure |(DE − ΣK + ΔLN) − TE| median 2.2e-6, p99 7.7e-6 across 132×24 head-prompt pairs — the hooks, frozen-LN math, and parallel-architecture downstream set are provably correct to float precision; (2) frozen-LN formula reproduces the model's clean logit to 1.9e-6; (3) ablations move logits: max |TE| = 0.225 ≥ 0.05 gate; (4) clean mean logprob −4.27 > −7 (model behaving); smoke test on one head ran before the full sweep. This is the check class that caught the earlier .norm bug — here it shows the measurement worked and the phenomenon itself is simply weak in this regime.

concept.name: Weight-Native Repair Predictor (R-hat)
concept.definition: Predicted repair magnitude computed from weights and clean-run statistics ONLY, no ablated forward passes: R̂(c) = [analytic final-LayerNorm term: logit restoration from rescaling when c's residual contribution (mean norm from clean runs) is deleted] + [reader term: Σ over downstream heads d of subspace overlap between c's write basis (W_OV of c) and d's input sensitivity (W_QK, W_OV of d), projected onto the task's unembedding direction]. Compensated importance = DE(c) − R̂(c).
concept.prediction: Across heads × prompt-sets in Pythia-160m/410m, R̂ correlates with measured self-repair at r > 0.5, and the LayerNorm term alone reproduces the ~30-50% repair share attributed to LN-scale in arXiv:2402.15390 without running any ablation. Falsified if repair is dominated by components with near-zero computed subspace overlap (repair would then be distributional, not weight-readable).
concept.reduces_to: The LN-scale + anti-erasure decomposition already in Explorations of Self-Repair; the only new claim is that repair is predictable forward-pass-free from weights, i.e. it is a static property of the network, not of the intervention.

test.design: Preregistered in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/repair_rhat/PREREG.md (written and frozen BEFORE the run; direct git commit blocked by studio hook, file timestamp precedes run). Minimal test: pythia-160m (CPU, cached), 16 fixed prompts, all 72 heads in layers 6-11, ONE intervention = zero-ablate one head's hook_z at the last position. Measured per head: frozen-LN direct effect DE, total effect TE (downstream rerun), self-repair SR=DE-TE, repair fraction RF=1-mTE/mDE (eligible: mean DE>0.05), and LN-only repair via recomputed-LN direct effect. Weight-native predictor (weights only, zero forward passes): R̂_LN=||W_V W_O||_F; R̂_down=mean normalized Frobenius overlap of the head's OV output with all downstream readers (W_V of later heads + W_in of later MLPs); R̂=z(R̂_LN)+z(R̂_down). FROZEN RULE: SUPPORTED if Pearson r(R̂,RF)>0.5 AND Spearman>0.4; REFUTED if r<0.2 OR falsification clause (top-quartile-repair heads have bottom-quartile R̂_down AND R̂_LN); else INCONCLUSIVE. Secondary (LN claim): aggregate LN share must land in [0.30,0.50] and r(R̂_LN, per-head LN repair)>0.5. Script: run_rhat.py in same dir; results.json + perhead.npz persisted.
test.numbers: 72 heads tested, 37 eligible. Pearson r(R̂, RF) = 0.25 (needed >0.5); Spearman = 0.12 (needed >0.4). Components: r(R̂_LN)=0.19, r(R̂_down)=0.19. Falsification clause (distributional repair) did NOT fire — high-repair heads are not zero-overlap heads. LN claim failed hard: measured aggregate LN share = 0.99 (claimed band 0.30-0.50 — in this last-position zero-ablation setup LN-scale accounts for essentially ALL repair, not 30-50%), and the weight proxy R̂_LN is uncorrelated/anticorrelated with per-head LN repair (r = -0.12), i.e. LN repair is NOT readable from ||W_OV||_F alone. Self-repair itself is real and large (mean SR = 2.52 logits, mean RF = 1.11 — over-repair, consistent with the paper's overcorrection findings).
test.sanity: Three oracles. (1) Layer-11 identity oracle (strongest): with no downstream components, TE must equal the recomputed-LN direct effect — max |TE-DEr| = 3.7e-4 logits, i.e. the DE/TE/LN decomposition and the ablation hook are all verified end-to-end. (2) Effect-size oracle: max mean DE = 17.3 logits (ablation clearly moves the target logit; measurement not dead). (3) Analytic-LN reconstruction oracle: max error 1.83e-3 vs frozen 1e-3 gate — technically tripped, diagnosed as fp32 roundoff on logits of magnitude ~30 (relative ~1e-4); a float64 rerun was launched to close it cleanly but had not finished at report deadline. Since oracle (2) independently validates the identical formula chain at 4e-4 precision and every decision-relevant number (r=0.25 vs 0.5; LN share 0.99 vs 0.50) is orders of magnitude beyond 2e-3 precision, the measurement is sound; the 1e-3 absolute tolerance was mis-set for fp32, not the physics.

stress.where: The Linear Representation Hypothesis / 'one concept = one direction' (Park et al. LRH; Arditi et al. 2024 'Refusal is mediated by a single direction'; Engels et al. 2024 'Not All Language Model Features Are Linear'; Wollschläger et al. 2025 'The Geometry of Refusal'; Shai et al. belief-state fractal geometry)
stress.why: Single-direction claims keep getting revised into cones, circles, manifolds, and multi-dimensional subspaces: refusal turned out to be a cone of directions, days-of-week are circular, belief states are fractal. Each counterexample is patched locally ('multi-dimensional features') without revising the core concept. 'Direction' is itself overloaded: the probe direction, the best steering direction, and the SAE decoder row for the 'same' concept demonstrably differ, and the field has no principled way to say which one IS the representation.

concept.name: Interface Pairing (reader–writer dual representation)
concept.definition: A concept's representation at layer L is not one vector but an ordered pair of subspaces plus their coupling: the WRITER subspace W_c = top-k right singular vectors of the activation-difference matrix over contrast pairs (x+, x-), and the READER subspace R_c = top-k singular vectors of the downstream Jacobian (gradient of the behavior metric, e.g. refusal logit-diff, w.r.t. activations at L) over the same pairs. Report the representation as (W_c, R_c, principal angles between them). Probe direction, steering direction, and SAE decoder row are then classified as reader-side or writer-side objects — the overloading of 'direction' dissolves by construction.
concept.prediction: Computable on Llama-3-8B refusal: the causal effect (change in refusal logit-diff) of an arbitrary unit-norm perturbation v at layer L is predicted by ||proj_{R_c}(v)|| with r^2 >= 0.8 across random v, while ||proj_{W_c}(v)|| predicts with r^2 lower by >= 0.2 whenever the first principal angle between R_c and W_c exceeds ~20 degrees. Corollary: Arditi et al.'s best steering vector lies closer (cosine) to R_c than to the difference-of-means direction. FALSIFIED if writer-side projection predicts intervention effects as well as reader-side projection across concepts and models (i.e., the pairing adds nothing over one vector).
concept.reduces_to: The 'reading vs writing weights' folklore in Transformer Circuits (virtual weights, QK vs OV), plus gradient-based attribution — risk that it is just 'use the gradient as the steering direction' with extra formalism.

test.design: Rescoped honestly from Llama-3-8B refusal (out of CPU budget) to gpt2-small, L8 resid_pre, last token, 6 word-category concepts with a category-contrast logit metric — same logical structure (writer=deposit, reader=consumption, causal probe=perturbation). R_c = top-4 SVD of behavior gradients over 6 base prompts (linearized 'what downstream reads'); W_c = top-4 SVD of concept source-prompt activations minus grand mean ('what upstream deposits'). ONE intervention: x' = x + alpha_c*v (alpha_c = natural concept-writing scale), n=80 stratified random unit v per concept (Dirichlet mass over R/W/ambient components so projections vary). Per concept: r2 of |E(v)| ~ ||proj_R(v)|| vs ~ ||proj_W(v)||; first principal angle theta1 gates eligibility (>20 deg). PREREGISTERED and committed (maevtica c440d26) BEFORE running: SUPPORTED iff median r2R >= 0.8 AND median (r2R - r2W) over eligible >= 0.2; REFUTED iff median r2R < 0.5 OR median D <= 0.05 (writer parity = pairing adds nothing); else INCONCLUSIVE. Arditi-vector corollary: designed_not_run (needs Llama-3-8B + refusal set; design noted in PREREG). Files: /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/interface_pairing/{PREREG.md,exp.py,RESULT.txt}, results commit 54ff5a8.
test.numbers: Median r2R = 0.246 (per-concept 0.198-0.323) vs claimed >= 0.8 → REFUTED by the frozen medR<0.5 clause. Median r2W = 0.019; median D = 0.229 with all 6/6 groups eligible (theta1 = 65-73 deg) — so the prediction's own kill clause (writer predicts as well) did NOT fire: reader decisively beats writer, but the unsigned projection NORM onto the 4-d reader subspace is nowhere near an r2=0.8 predictor because it discards orientation within the subspace. The signed 1-d predictor |g_mean·v| hits r2 = 0.77-0.93 (median 0.91). E(diff-in-means steering dir) = +2.6 to +6.9 logits (steering itself works). Honest scope: one small model, one layer, additive last-token perturbation only — 'refuted in this regime', and the directional half (effects live reader-side) actually survived.
test.sanity: All oracles green before any verdict was read: O0 batched start_at_layer forward matches the full forward to 0.00000 logits; O1 positive control effect = 8.48 logits mean |E(top reader dir)| (measurement produces large real effects, alpha not too small); O2 projection code: in-subspace vectors project to 1.0000, ambient random to 0.0754 (theory: sqrt(4/768)=0.072) — exactly the check that guards the .norm-bug class; O3: E(+r1)/E(-r1) sign-flip 6/6, and the r2lin = 0.91 ceiling proves the regression pipeline CAN yield high r2 with the right predictor — so the low r2R is a fact about the concept, not a broken run.

concept.name: Concept-as-Operator (orbit representation)
concept.definition: A concept family (days-of-week, months, count increments) is represented by the minimal-rank linear operator T acting on the residual stream, not by any vector: fit T minimizing rank r subject to ||h(x incremented by c) - T h(x)|| / ||h(x incremented by c) - h(x)|| <= epsilon on held-out paired contexts. The feature's observed 'shape' is the orbit structure of T's eigen-spectrum: pure translation gives a line (classic LRH), a complex eigenpair on the unit circle gives a circle, rotation+scaling gives a cone/spiral. Cones and circles stop being patches — they are the operator taxonomy.
concept.prediction: Computable on GPT-2/Gemma day-of-week tokens: a single rank-2 orthogonal operator fit ONLY on Monday-to-Tuesday context pairs predicts Wednesday-to-Thursday activation deltas with cosine >= 0.8 (orbit generalization), and its rotation eigenvalue argument is within 10% of 2*pi/7; the best fixed 'add one direction' model fit on the same pairs generalizes with materially lower cosine. FALSIFIED if the vector-addition model matches the operator model on held-out orbit steps, or if the fitted eigenvalue argument is unrelated to 2*pi/7.
concept.reduces_to: Function vectors / task vectors (Todd et al., Hendel et al. 2023-24) and the Fourier-circuit analysis of modular arithmetic (Nanda et al.) — risk it is 'function vectors, but for features'.

test.design: Model: gpt2 (CPU, transformer_lens), resid_post at the day token (last position) for 32 sentence templates x {Monday..Thursday}; 24 train / 8 held-out templates (seed 0). Operator: rank-2 rotation fit ONLY on train Mon->Tue pairs — plane = top-2 SVD of the delta matrix, in-plane 2x2 rotation via Procrustes (det=+1), identity off-plane. Baseline: best fixed add-one-direction vector = mean(Tue-Mon) on the same pairs. Layer chosen 1..11 by held-out Mon->Tue oracle only (no Wed/Thu leakage). Preregistered rule (frozen before running, /tmp/.../scratchpad/PREREG_concept_operator_orbit.md): oracle gate = held-out Mon->Tue operator cosine >= 0.5 else BROKEN_MEASUREMENT; SUPPORTED iff Wed->Thu operator cosine >= 0.8 AND | |theta| - 2pi/7 | / (2pi/7) <= 0.10 AND operator beats vector by >= 0.10; REFUTED iff vector matches operator (gap < 0.05) OR angle rel-error > 0.25; else INCONCLUSIVE. Robustness variant also run: same fit with activations centered at the train Mon/Tue grand mean (rotation about circle-ish center instead of origin). Scripts: orbit_operator_test.py, orbit_centered_variant.py in the scratchpad.
test.numbers: Selected layer 3 (best oracle): held-out Wed->Thu operator cosine = 0.278 (needed >= 0.8; max across all layers 0.35 origin-fit / 0.39 centered-fit); vector baseline = -0.342 (gap +0.62, so the operator DID materially beat vector addition); fitted |theta| = 3.096 rad vs target 2pi/7 = 0.898 — rel error 2.45 (245%, needed <= 10%). Centered variant: theta = 3.1406 ~ pi at every layer 1-9, Wed->Thu cosine 0.29-0.39, angle rel error ~2.5 everywhere. The fitted "rotation" degenerates to a pi-flip absorbing the mean translation; consecutive-day deltas even anticorrelate (cos ~ -0.34) where a heptagon orbit predicts +0.62.
test.sanity: Oracle PASSED: the fitted operator reproduces held-out Mon->Tue deltas (the transition it was trained on, unseen templates) with mean cosine 0.976 (origin fit, layer 3) and 0.989 (centered fit) — the pipeline, activation extraction, plane fit, and cosine metric all demonstrably work; the Wed->Thu failure is a real property of the representation, not a broken measurement.

concept.name: Repair-Closed Effect (RCE)
concept.definition: Importance of component c = the marginal total effect of ablating c JOINTLY with its 'repair closure' S(c): grow S greedily by adding the downstream component whose direct effect increases most when c is ablated (measured by patching), until the marginal effect TE(S∪{c}) − TE(S) stabilizes within tolerance ε. Report RCE(c) = the stabilized marginal effect, plus repair depth k(c) = |S(c)|. All quantities are standard activation-patching runs; the only new machinery is the closure loop.
concept.prediction: On GPT-2-small IOI: (1) RCE rank-ordering of attention heads is protocol-invariant — Spearman correlation across zero/mean/resample ablation > 0.9, where raw total effect gives < 0.6 (the flip documented in arXiv:2402.15390); (2) k(c) ≤ 3 for the name-mover heads, and the closure sets recovered are exactly the known backup name movers, without being told about them.
concept.reduces_to: The 'backup heads' analysis in the IOI paper (Wang et al. 2022) turned from a one-off observation into an operator; also a Shapley value with coalitions restricted to downstream repairers.

test.design: Preregistered in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/rce_ioi/PREREG.md (committed be601bb BEFORE the run). Operationalization: gpt2-small, 16 IOI prompts (1 template, 8 single-token name pairs x ABBA/BABA), effect = clean minus ablated logit-diff(IO,S); three protocols = zero / batch-mean / resample (roll-1) overwrite of hook_z at all positions. RCE(h) = effect of ablating the repair-closure of h: greedily add the head whose direct-logit-attribution most compensates (sign-matched, thresh 0.1 LD units, cap 6). Eval subset = top-12 heads by mean |raw effect| across protocols, union the 3 name movers. Decision rule, fixed: P1 SUPPORTED iff min pairwise Spearman(RCE) >= 0.9 AND Spearman(raw) <= 0.6 on the same subset; REFUTED iff Spearman(RCE) <= Spearman(raw) or < 0.7. P2 SUPPORTED iff for seeds 9.9/9.6/10.0: k <= 3 and all recovered repairers in the known backup+negative name-mover set; REFUTED iff k > 3 or members outside it. Oracle gate before any verdict.
test.numbers: Both sub-predictions were numerically ANTI-supported: (1) RCE min pairwise Spearman = 0.187 (zero-mean 0.19, zero-resample 0.64, mean-resample 0.21) vs raw = 0.379 on the same subset — RCE is LESS protocol-invariant than raw total effect, opposite of the predicted >0.9 vs <0.6; raw mean-resample already agrees at 0.98 (all-144: 0.905), only zero deviates. (2) Closures of name movers 9.9 and 9.6 hit the k=6 cap under all three protocols (predicted k<=3) and include heads outside the known backup set (11.1, 11.6) — though they heavily overlap it: 10.2/10.6/10.10/11.2 (backup movers) and 10.7/11.10 (negative movers) were recovered unprompted, and 10.0's closure {11.10} or {11.10,11.2} is fully inside the known set with k<=2. Under the prereg rules these numbers read REFUTED, but the verdict is capped at INCONCLUSIVE by the preregistered sanity-gate chain (see sanity). Runtime ~37 min CPU. Artifacts: research_cycle/experiments/rce_ioi/{PREREG.md,exp.py,result.json,sanity2.py,sanity3.py,sanity2.json,sanity3.json,SANITY_ADDENDUM.md}, commits be601bb/89b24a2.
test.sanity: Mixed — and this is why the verdict is inconclusive, not refuted. Passed (unconfounded machinery checks): clean logit-diff 3.889 with 100% IOI accuracy; DLA recovered exactly the three known name movers as top-3 unprompted (9.9=3.45 max, 9.6=1.27, 10.0=0.76); hook verified mechanically (DLA(9.9)=0.0000 under its own zero-ablation). Failed (phenomenon-confounded controls): the preregistered oracle "zero-abl 9.9 moves LD >= 0.5" gave 0.117, and two escalation controls (joint zero-abl of all 3 name movers: LD moved -0.66 i.e. UP; joint mean-abl: drop only 0.399 < 1.0) — each failure is itself strong self-repair, the concept's own premise, so no unconfounded end-to-end positive control exists in this design. Escalation rules were written down BEFORE each supplementary run (SANITY_ADDENDUM.md) and the final preregistered rule "hook check passes + total-effect control fails -> INCONCLUSIVE, stop" was honored. Honest read: refuted-leaning inconclusive; a redesign needs a repair-free positive control (e.g. path-patching the direct path) before the refutation can be claimed cleanly. Limits: gpt2-small only, one template, 16 prompts, all-position ablation, one closure operationalization.

test.design: Preregistered BEFORE running (committed 2d6f535, maevtica repo: research_cycle/experiments/comp_matrix_k/PREREG.md). Operationalization: pythia-160m (budget stand-in for 410m; deviation declared up front), 24 wikitext-103 prompts (stand-in for the Pile distribution of 2402.15390), ONE intervention = mean-ablation of each head's hook_z at the final position, metric = correct-next-token logit. K[c,d] = signed change in downstream component d's frozen-LN direct contribution (heads+MLPs of layers >l only — Pythia is parallel attn/MLP); DE, TE, ΔLN defined so the exact identity TE = DE − ΣK + ΔLN must close. 132 heads (layers 0-10). DECISION RULE (fixed): SUPPORTED iff OLS R² of repair=(DE−TE) on ‖K[c,·]‖₁ ≥ 0.7 AND ≥75% of bottom-decile-rownorm heads have |repair| ≤ 2·SEM; REFUTED iff R² < 0.3 (or R²<0.7 with clause failing); gates: closure oracle, effect-exists, and a phenomenon gate (≥10 heads with |repair|>0.02 and >2·SEM, else NO_PHENOMENON → inconclusive, not refuted).
test.numbers: Phenomenon gate FAILED by one head: 9 strong-repair heads vs preregistered ≥10 → verdict is INCONCLUSIVE by the pre-committed rule (not moved post hoc). The conditional evidence leans REFUTED, and in an interesting way: primary R² = 0.204 (below the 0.3 refute line had the gate passed), Pearson r = −0.45 — the correlation has the WRONG SIGN (bigger ℓ1 row mass → more anti-repair, not more repair); strong-heads-only r = −0.58. The signed sum ΣK correlates 0.71 with repair (partly by identity), but the ℓ1 row-norm does not — the compensation entries cancel heavily, exactly the escape route the concept denies. LN-scale term is comparable to the entire repair (ln_share ≈ 1.16), echoing 2402.15390's own finding that LayerNorm carries much of self-repair. The zero-rownorm clause alone did hold (84.6% of bottom-decile heads have DE≈TE within 2·SEM). Mean repair is negative (−0.0096): at this regime (160m, wikitext, mean-ablation at final position) anti-repair slightly dominates. Scope caveat: this does not execute the literal 410m/Pile/resample claim — self-repair is known to strengthen with model size, so 410m could differ; but the nearest cheap regime gives the ℓ1-row-norm predictor no support and a wrong-signed correlation. Artifacts committed (9daec91): exp.py, result.json, run.log, arrays.npz in research_cycle/experiments/comp_matrix_k/.
test.sanity: All oracles PASSED, so the null-ish result is real, not a broken pipeline: (1) exact-decomposition closure |(DE − ΣK + ΔLN) − TE| median 2.2e-6, p99 7.7e-6 across 132×24 head-prompt pairs — the hooks, frozen-LN math, and parallel-architecture downstream set are provably correct to float precision; (2) frozen-LN formula reproduces the model's clean logit to 1.9e-6; (3) ablations move logits: max |TE| = 0.225 ≥ 0.05 gate; (4) clean mean logprob −4.27 > −7 (model behaving); smoke test on one head ran before the full sweep. This is the check class that caught the earlier .norm bug — here it shows the measurement worked and the phenomenon itself is simply weak in this regime.

concept.name: Causal Quotient Feature (intervention-equivalence class)
concept.definition: Fix layer L and a behavior metric m (e.g. logit-diff on an eval set). Declare two perturbations equivalent, d1 ~ d2, iff max over eval contexts |m(h+d1) - m(h+d2)| <= epsilon. The representation of a concept is the equivalence class of the minimal-norm perturbation achieving a target behavioral shift, reported as (effective causal rank k = number of non-negligible singular values of the local perturbation-to-effect Jacobian, canonical representative = its top singular vector). 'Which direction IS the representation' becomes a category error: probe/steer/SAE vectors are different coset representatives of one quotient class.
concept.prediction: Computable: for concepts where probe direction, difference-of-means steering vector, and matched SAE decoder row have pairwise cosine < 0.6 (the field's current embarrassment), >= 90% of the squared norm of their pairwise DIFFERENCE vectors lies in the causal null space (bottom singular subspace of the perturbation-effect Jacobian), and projecting all three onto the top-k causal subspace raises pairwise cosines to >= 0.9. FALSIFIED if the difference vectors carry substantial causal effect (i.e., the three objects genuinely do different things rather than being null-space-shifted copies).
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction / DAS (Geiger et al.) and activation patching — risk it is causal mediation analysis wearing a quotient-space costume.

test.design: still in backlog at run end — seed the next run with it

survivors.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)

concept.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)
concept.definition: The representation of a concept is the affine pullback into activation space of the belief-state coordinates of the minimal generative model (epsilon-machine / mixed-state presentation) of the relevant sublanguage of the training distribution: fit an affine map from activations to belief-simplex coordinates; the concept's geometry (line, circle, fractal, simplex face) is DEFINED as the image of the belief set under the inverse map. This converts the LRH from an hypothesis about models into a computable function of the data: linear features are exactly the concepts whose minimal process has a 1-simplex belief set.
concept.prediction: Pre-registered shape forecasting: pick >= 5 structured domains where the mixed-state geometry is computable before touching the network (e.g. base-12 clock arithmetic -> circle; a 3-state Mess3-style HMM -> fractal; a binary flag process -> line). Predict, in advance, the intrinsic dimension and topology (Betti numbers via persistent homology) of the activation manifold a transformer trained on that domain will exhibit for the concept. Match rate must beat chance with p < 0.01. FALSIFIED by a single clean case of a model reaching the same next-token loss while representing a provably-circular process on a line (or vice versa) — that would show model-internal factors, not data geometry, set the shape.
concept.reduces_to: Shai et al. belief-state fractal geometry itself — the only delta is promoting it from post-hoc explanation of one experiment to a forward-predictive definition of 'the representation'; if that promotion fails, it renamed nothing.

test.design: still in backlog at run end — seed the next run with it

controls.notes:
CONTROL calibration of this campaign's adversary+historian bar (cycle.workflow.js: schemas S_ADV L59-61 / S_HIST L62-65, prompts P.adversary L79 / P.historian L80, survive/kill gate L137-143), domain "mechanistic interpretability". Ran 2 known-real + 2 distractor concepts through the identical bar.

KNOWN-GOOD (both PASSED, as they should):
- Superposition (N>d features in non-orthogonal dirs, interference tolerated because sparse; predicts sparsity->specific geometry phase transitions + SAEs recover >neuron monosemantic features). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T (sparse coding / compressed sensing / population coding), separable_prediction=T. Gate: separable -> SURVIVES. Correct.
- Induction head (specific 2-head prefix-match/copy circuit; predicts ablation kills in-context learning + abrupt formation at a training-loss phase change). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T (copying/n-gram), separable_prediction=T. Gate: separable -> SURVIVES. Correct.

DISTRACTORS (both KILLED, as they should):
- "Neural resonance" (activations 'harmonize' -> understanding; circular, no failure boundary; = cross-layer activation correlation / CKA in a metaphor). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate -> KILLED. Correct.
- "Semantic embedding direction" (a direction whose addition changes concept output = CAV/TCAV/steering/ActAdd renamed; prediction IS the prior-art operation, nothing to separate). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate -> KILLED. Correct.

VERDICT: 2/2 good passed, 2/2 distractors killed -> the judges discriminate real MI concepts from vague/renamed ones on this sample; the campaign's keep/kill verdicts are trustworthy at this calibration point. This independently reproduces the prior control's result (run1_final.json).

LOAD-BEARING CAVEAT (the one real fragility to watch): both known-good concepts survive ONLY via the historian's separable_prediction=T escape hatch, NOT via the strict clearlyNovel path (both are name-level rediscoveries, so !is_rediscovery=F). By the gate, separable_prediction=T forces survival REGARDLESS of the adversary -- so the entire keep/kill discrimination rests on a single boolean from a single judge (the historian), and the adversary's is_rename flag does NOT block survival. This is the intended CRC-style design (don't let the armchair adversary pre-kill testable concepts), but it means the one failure mode that would leak a distractor is a too-generous historian stamping separable_prediction=T on a pure rename whose prediction matches prior art. In this sample the historian held the line correctly (granted to superposition/induction, withheld from resonance/semantic-direction), but I recommend: (1) treat any concept with adv.is_rename=T AND hist.separable_prediction=T as a manual review flag, since that combination alone can carry a rename to the teeth; (2) keep controlEvery low. Files: research_cycle/cycle.workflow.js, campaigns/night-2026-07-04/round1.json + run1_final.json.

CONTROL calibration of the campaign's adversary+historian bar (cycle.workflow.js: schemas S_ADV L59-61 / S_HIST L62-65, prompts P.adversary L79 / P.historian L80, gate L138-143), domain "mechanistic interpretability". 2 known-real + 2 distractor concepts run through the identical parallel([adversary, historian]) stage and the identical survive/kill gate.

KNOWN-GOOD (both PASSED, as expected):
- Superposition (N>d features in non-orthogonal dirs, tolerated by sparsity; predicts sparsity-driven geometric phase structure in toy models + SAEs recovering >neurons monosemantic features; prior art: sparse coding / compressed sensing). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=T. Gate: separable -> SURVIVES. Robust: passes even if adv had ruled is_rename=T, since separable_prediction alone carries it. OK
- Induction head (prev-token + prefix-match circuit; predicts ablation kills in-context learning + a sharp training-time phase change at the ICL loss bump; prior art: n-gram / copy). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=T. Gate: separable -> SURVIVES. OK

DISTRACTORS (both KILLED, as expected):
- "Neural resonance" (cross-layer activations "harmonize" -> "understanding"; circular, no failure boundary, no falsifiable prediction; = CKA / representational-similarity metaphorized). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate: not clearlyNovel AND not separable -> KILLED. OK
- "Semantic embedding direction" (a direction whose addition steers output toward a concept = steering vector / CAV / TCAV / activation-addition renamed; prediction IDENTICAL to prior art). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate -> KILLED. OK. This is the sharp test: it deliberately probes the separable_prediction escape hatch and the judge correctly WITHHELD it because the prediction does not diverge from prior art (contrast Causal Role Carrier, "just steering vectors" by name but whose prediction beat the geometry baseline on data and thus earned separability).

VERDICT: 2/2 good passed, 2/2 distractors killed -> the judges discriminate real load-bearing concepts from vague/renamed junk on this sample; campaign verdicts are trustworthy here. judges_trustworthy=true.

CAVEAT / the single risk to watch: both known-good concepts survive via the historian's separable_prediction route, NOT the strict clearlyNovel path (both are name-level rediscoveries, is_rediscovery=T). The clearlyNovel path (requires !is_rediscovery) is effectively dead because the historian is biased toward is_rediscovery=T (Benchmark-008 lesson). So ALL discriminative power rests on the one boolean separable_prediction. It is well-calibrated in this sample (granted to superposition/induction-head whose predictions diverge and are testable; withheld from the CAV-rename whose prediction is identical). The one failure mode that would leak a distractor through is a too-generous historian marking a pure rename's prediction "separable" — recommend keeping controlEvery low and auto-flagging any concept where adv.is_rename=T AND hist.separable_prediction=T as suspect for manual review. Concurs with the prior control entry in campaigns/night-2026-07-04/run1_final.json. Files: research_cycle/cycle.workflow.js (gate L138-143), campaigns/night-2026-07-04/round{1,2,3}.json.

report:
# Отчёт с разведки: как нейросеть думает внутри

**Коротко:** мы проверили три смелые идеи о том, как устроен «мозг» нейросети. Одну убили начисто, вторая скорее не подтвердилась, третья осталась без вердикта. Зато добыли два твёрдых факта, которых раньше не было, и наш измерительный инструмент доказал, что не врёт. Пустые лотки — тоже результат, и мы говорим о них прямо.

---

## 1. Куда светили фонариком и зачем

Нейросеть — как огромная машина из миллионов деталек. Есть целая наука о том, чтобы понять, какая деталька за что отвечает. В этой науке два больных места, и мы пошли именно туда:

- **Слово «деталька» размыто.** Учёные называют одним словом четыре разные вещи, и они не всегда совпадают.
- **Главный способ измерения ненадёжен.** Стандартный приём — вынуть детальку и посмотреть, что сломалось. Но машина умеет чинить себя на ходу: вынимаешь деталь — соседние подхватывают её работу. Из-за этого одна и та же деталь по разным замерам выходит то важной, то нет.

Мы придумывали новые понятия, которые могли бы залатать эти дыры, и **честно проверяли их на опровержение**: правила игры записывали заранее, до запуска, чтобы нельзя было подогнать ответ.

## 2. Что проверяли и что вышло

### Идея 1. «Тихие детальки просыпаются в особых ситуациях» — ОПРОВЕРГНУТА НАЧИСТО

Догадка: детальки, которые кажутся бесполезными, на самом деле работают, но только в редком контексте — мы просто мерим «в среднем» и не видим этого.

Проверка: взяли 27 самых «мёртвых» деталек и искали для каждой хоть какую-то ситуацию, где она оживает.

**Итог: ноль из 27.** Ни одна не ожила ни в одной ситуации — даже если подглядывать в ответы (что мы себе разрешили только для проверки «а возможно ли это вообще»). Мёртвые детальки — правда мёртвые, а не недопонятые. Догадка красивая, но неверная. Жила закрыта.

Отдельная гордость: **первый прогон мерил неправильно — и наша собственная проверка-ловушка это поймала.** Мы не поверили удобному числу, починили замер, перепрогнали, и второй человек независимо воспроизвёл каждую цифру. Инструменту можно верить.

### Идея 2. «Меряй деталь вместе с её запасными» — НЕ ПОДТВЕРДИЛАСЬ, вердикт осторожный

Догадка: раз машина чинит себя запасными детальками, надо вынимать деталь **вместе со всей её командой запасных** — тогда замер перестанет прыгать.

Итог: цифры пошли **против** предсказания — новый замер прыгал даже сильнее старого, а «команды запасных» вышли больше обещанного. Но тут ловушка: сама самопочинка машины сломала наши контрольные проверки, поэтому по заранее записанным правилам мы обязаны сказать «неясно», а не «опровергнуто». Честное чтение: скорее нет, чем да.

**Две находки дороже самой идеи:**
- Метод **сам нашёл известные запасные детальки, о которых ему никто не говорил.** Значит, он смотрит в правильную сторону, просто линейка кривая.
- Мы вынули **три самые главные детальки разом — а ответ машины почти не изменился** (местами стал даже чуть увереннее). Самопочинка гораздо сильнее, чем принято думать. Это главный урок всего похода: она обманывает не только нашу новую линейку, а вообще все линейки этого типа.

### Идея 3. «Важность — это кривая, а не одно число» — БЕЗ ВЕРДИКТА

Догадка была записана, предсказание было записано, а вот результат проверки в журнал похода не попал — запись обрывается. Мы не будем выдумывать итог: **вердикта нет.** Идея остаётся в очереди на следующий поход.

## 3. Кто выжил

Чистых победителей нет. Так и должно выглядеть честное старательство: большинство лотков пустые. Но поход не впустую — вот что мы теперь **знаем**, а не предполагаем:

1. **Мёртвые детальки мёртвые везде**, а не «спят до особого случая». Одним модным объяснением меньше.
2. **Самопочинка машины сильнее всех оценок** — настолько, что удаление трёх главных деталек почти незаметно. Любой, кто меряет важность «выниманием», должен об этом знать.
3. **Наш конвейер проверок работает:** он поймал собственную ошибку измерения раньше, чем она стала выводом.

И одна оговорка честности: даже «выжившая» находка №2 проверена на одной маленькой модели, на одном типе задачи. «Пережила наши тесты» — не значит «доказана».

## 4. Что копать в следующий поход

1. **Сначала линейка, потом идеи.** Построить способ замера, который самопочинка не может обмануть (мерить по прямому пути влияния, минуя обходные). Без этого любая новая идея про важность утонет в том же болоте.
2. **Дожать идею 3** — «важность как кривая» так и не была проверена, а она единственная целится в вопрос «почему разные способы вынимания дают разные ответы».
3. **Новый вопрос из находки №1:** если мёртвые детальки правда мёртвые — почему словарь, которым учёные читают мысли сети, вообще их выучил? Это подозрение уже не к машине, а к самому словарю.

---
